title: dianthus hispanicus subsp. langeanus
intro: Dianthus hispanicus subsp. langeanus, commonly known as the Iberian pink or small pink, is a species of flowering plant in the carnation family, Dianthaceae. Traditionally, this plant has been used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it a potential treatment for wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of Dianthus hispanicus subsp. langeanus are also attributed to its ability to reduce fever, relieve headaches, and support respiratory health. In herbal preparations, the plant can be used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
